profile.name: James
profile.role_in_household: Single household head; primary earner and sole decision-maker
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate-to-high: comfortable with investing concepts and account types, but tends to underweight cash-flow mechanics and the true cost of high-interest debt until it’s spelled out.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Aggressive by preference; leans toward staying invested and looking for growth/“smart” moves, and can resist selling investments to pay down debt unless the trade-off is framed clearly.
profile.communication_style: Direct but a bit rushed; uses qualifiers like “I think” and “roughly,” may give round numbers first and then correct himself. Asks pointed questions when something doesn’t add up, but admits when he doesn’t remember exact dates/terms.
emotional_tendencies: stress spikes around monthly bills and due dates
profile.decision_making_style: Decides quickly once he sees a simple comparison (e.g., interest rate vs expected return); prefers 2–3 options with a recommendation rather than an open-ended discussion.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously open: willing to share details and take guidance, but wants to verify logic and dislikes feeling judged or sold to.
internal_biases: optimism bias about future income/market returns
present bias (prioritizes immediate cash-flow relief over long-term optimization until prompted)
anchoring to account balances rather than interest rates
hidden_concerns: worries that debt is undermining his progress despite having sizable investable assets
concerned about being “house poor” given the large monthly housing payment
quietly anxious about health/lifestyle risks and how long he can maintain his current pace
